sessions.lever: statement-reorder levers (a2-before-sqrt, disc operand order, dy hoist) all measured-negative

reviewer.evidence: No candidate saved (no floor improvement); nothing to review.

remaining_gap.summary: Pure register-rotation: both builds are 168 insns (structure already correct), the 14 regfix rules are ALL `subst` register renames with NO insert/delete/reorder. The diff is a dense coupled $v0/$v1/$t1/$a1/$a2 rotation across the projectile-angle compute chain (dy, disc=arg2^2+arg3*dy2, sq=func_8007E11C(disc<<10), a0=((a2+/-sq)*dist)/dy2/32, then a0*dx/dist & a0*dz/dist). A mid-function CALL (func_8007E11C, the sqrt) forces callee-save decisions that couple the whole allocation.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: All 14 are register-rename substs; the rotation is a fixpoint (each lever tried makes it worse, not better).

next_hypotheses: Instrumented cc1 ALLOCDBG/PRIODBG ([[register-alloc-deep-dive]]) to read the allocno-priority tiebreak driving the $v0/$v1 swap at the `dy` subu and the disc mflo pair — hand levers cascade because of the mid-function sqrt call's callee-save coupling.
The dy result ($v0 target / $v1 mine) is the FIRST diff and may be the rotation root; a lever that flips ONLY dy without disturbing the call's save/restore (not yet found) could cascade-fix.
Likely the register-alloc-pure-c 'confirmed limits' class (coupled rotation at matched insn count, like marionation_Exec) -> canonical-asm review candidate if clean levers stay negative.

rejected_forms.reason: Forces a2 callee-save across the call; +1 worse.
Reassociates the mult/add chain; insn count drops to 167, large divergence.
